summary: End-to-end testing of Email/Password JWT Authentication. Backend (pytest, 11 tests) and Frontend (Playwright) both PASS. Register/login/me endpoints behave correctly; React AuthProvider persists JWT in localStorage under 'careerverse:auth', re-validates via /auth/me on reload, and Navbar reflects signed-in state ('Hi, QA' + 'Sign out').

frontend_test_results.login_invalid_credentials_inline_error: PASS ("Invalid email or password" from backend 401)
frontend_test_results.session_persistence_after_reload: PASS (token retained, no redirect to /login, navbar still authed)
frontend_test_results.register_short_password_validation: PASS ("Password must be at least 8 characters")

critical_code_review_comments: Auth code is clean and minimal: bcrypt rounds=12, HS256 JWT, 30-day TTL, Pydantic EmailStr + min_length=8 validation, proper 401/409 error semantics, _id stripped before return.
JWT_SECRET falls back to 'dev-secret' if env var missing (acceptable for preview, but should be enforced/required in production).
Login.jsx validate() lacks explicit min-length check on password (rejection happens only via server 422/401). Functionally fine since the register flow does enforce it client-side.
test_credentials: Seeded user works as documented: test.student@careerverse.io / Test@1234 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md).
seed_data_creation: Tests created throwaway accounts qa+<timestamp>@careerverse.io (3 such users left in users collection — safe, idempotent for re-runs since each uses unique timestamp).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auth (register/login/me + React AuthProvider + localStorage 'careerverse:auth') is fully verified PASS. The Navbar already exposes user-aware UI (greeting + Sign out). There are intentionally NO protected frontend route guards (per user instructions). For follow-up features, leverage signed-in seeded user and the existing /api/auth/login + Bearer-token pattern. The backend_test.py is reusable as regression suite (run with -n0 to avoid xdist breaking shared module-level state).
